  "findings": [
    "DP impulse lines installed with incorrect slope for the process service.",
    "Gas service impulse lines tap from bottom of pipe, creating liquid collection points.",
    "Liquid service impulse lines tap from top of pipe, creating gas/vapor collection points.",
    "Installation does not comply with manufacturer instructions or API RP 551 process measurement standards."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately isolate and remove transmitter from service until corrected.",
    "Re-pipe impulse lines with proper slope and tap orientation per manufacturer and API RP 551.",
    "Purge, leak-test, and re-commission the transmitter loop."
  ],
  "rationale": "DP transmitter impulse line slope and tap location are critical to accurate measurement. In gas service, lines must slope upward to the transmitter or seal pot to allow condensate to drain back to the pipe; tapping from the bottom creates a liquid trap, leading to measurement error and potential corrosion or freezing. In liquid service, lines must slope upward to the process connection to allow gas to vent back; tapping from the top creates a gas pocket. These defects are classic installation errors that render the measurement unreliable. The asset is in service, so the condition constitutes a latent non-compliance and active degradation of measurement functionality.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 551",
    "Transmitter manufacturer installation manual"
  ]
}
